name: mass-search
description: Keyless, local-hardware mass web search + research synthesis. No API keys, no usage limits.
long_description: Mass Search turns a question into a whole research campaign: a local LLM fans it into many diverse queries, harvests them in parallel across free/keyless resolvers (DuckDuckGo, Wikipedia, Hacker News, Crossref, OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, GitHub, StackExchange, and an optional local SearXNG), distills each result set, and synthesizes a direct, source-grounded answer. Everything runs on your own hardware with no API keys, so there are no per-call usage limits. A per-host circuit breaker keeps your IP ban-safe. Node transport (bundled with Claude Desktop) drives a dependency-free stdlib Python engine, so it needs Python 3.9+ installed on your machine.

tools.name: mass_search
tools.description: Full local research campaign -> synthesized answer.

tools.name: read_campaign
tools.description: Reopen a saved campaign's report/corpus by slug.

python_path.title: Python command
python_path.description: How to launch Python (needs Python 3.9+ on your machine). Usually 'python'; use a full path or 'py' if that fails.

ollama_model.title: Local LLM model
ollama_model.description: e.g. hermes3:8b. Blank = first model found.

searxng_url.title: Local SearXNG URL (optional)
searxng_url.description: e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8888 to enable the SearXNG resolver.

contact_email.title: Contact email (optional)
contact_email.description: Put in the User-Agent + polite-pool mailto (Wikipedia/Crossref/OpenAlex ask for it).
